_meta.purpose: Image-edit pass — final delta from L9.5 keeper to L10 'Royal Elite'. Single-image chained input. Four beats: cabasset brim + gold diamond finial cap (preserves apex) + bigger pauldrons (volume bump on existing 2 plates) + fully gold longbow.
_meta.input_image_content: UnitProgression/Archer/out/v3/L9_5/composite/archer_L9_5_v<keeper>_composited.png (v3 chain, user picks keeper at runtime)
_meta.level_beat_doc: UnitProgression/Archer/Archer_Progression_Plan.md (L10 — Royal Elite; revised 2026-05-08 — cabasset brim + diamond cap restored)
_meta.input_strategy: Single-input chained (locked 2026-05-07). Sole input = the L9.5 composite. NO L1_Base second image.
history: v1 (2026-05-04): 2 adds (gold spike finial + fully gold bow).

v2_royal (2026-05-05): expanded to 11 deltas including helm beats (cabasset brim + diamond finial + crown spikelets + visor T-bar) plus body decoration (3-lame pauldrons, gauntlets, breastplate filigree, cape clasps, cartouche buckle, vambrace scrollwork, bow limb-tip scrollwork).
v3_apex_preserved (2026-05-05): apex-shrink fix — diamond CAPS the tall pointed apex, does not replace it.
v4_detail_economy (2026-05-07): MAJOR REWRITE per AD FeedbackV0. Dropped 9 of 11 v2_royal deltas including all helm beats. Replaced with 'gold spike finial replaces apex' + 'fully gold bow'. 2 adds.

v5_helm_partial_restore (2026-05-08): RESTORED two of the v2_royal helm beats per user feedback — the cabasset brim and the gold diamond finial cap (preserves the tall pointed apex). The other v2_royal helm beats (three crown spikelets along the trim band, vertical gold T-bar on the visor, gold visor perimeter trim) STAY CUT — too much surface ornament. The 'full gold spike finial replaces apex' from v4 is REPLACED by 'diamond CAPS the tall apex' (v3_apex_preserved pattern). Stays cut from v2_royal: breastplate filigree scrollwork, vambrace cuff scrollwork, decorative bow limb-tip scrollwork, cartouche-shaped buckle, gauntleted gloves, gold cape clasps, 3-lame pauldron expansion, crown spikelets, visor T-bar. Adds count 2 → 3.

v6_pauldron_volume_bump (2026-05-08): ADDED a fourth beat — pauldrons grow visibly bigger and chunkier for a mightier shoulder silhouette at the royal payoff. Volume bump on the EXISTING two-plate stack (upper cap dome grows taller and wider; lower lame extends ~1 inch further down the upper bicep). NO third lame added (per AD detail-economy 'wider plates, fewer of them' — grow the existing plates rather than fragment). NO rivets, NO leading-edge gold trim, NO surface ornament — pure volume. Adds count 3 → 4.

v7_boot_progression (2026-05-08): Per AD feedback — boots get a plate-only progression with a royal-payoff finishing beat. The L6 brass cuff trim + brass eyelets concept was rejected; the boot armor stack at L10 input is THREE STEEL PLATES (L6 toe cap + L9 heel cap + L9 ankle guard band). At L10 the boots gain THIN BUTTERY-GOLD EDGE LINES tracing the boundary of EACH of the three steel plates — material accent on existing armor, no new plate added, no surface trim bands. Adds count 4 → 5.

adds.title: HELM ADDS A SHORT FLARING CABASSET BRIM AT THE LOWER EDGE
adds.summary: Short flaring brim curves outward at the temple line and along the back of the neck (~1 inch wide all around), giving the helm the classic 16th-century cabasset / pikeman-helm silhouette. Tall pointed conical apex from the input image is preserved exactly.

adds.title: GOLD FACETED DIAMOND FINIAL CAPS THE TALL POINTED APEX
adds.summary: Small (~1.5 inch tall) faceted buttery-gold diamond ornament caps the tip of the helm's tall pointed conical apex. The apex from the input image is preserved in full — the diamond does NOT replace the apex, it CAPS it.

adds.title: PAULDRONS GROW BIGGER FOR A MIGHTIER SHOULDER SILHOUETTE
adds.summary: Volume bump on the existing two-plate steel pauldrons — the upper cap dome grows taller and wider, the lower lame extends roughly 1 inch further down the upper bicep. No third lame, no rivets, no gold trim — pure volume.

adds.title: FULLY BUTTERY-GOLD LONGBOW
adds.summary: The outer-50%-gilt longbow limbs from the input image FINISH their conversion to fully gold: the warm wood-brown inner-50% portion of each limb is now also polished buttery-yellow gold, matching the existing outer-50%-gilt portion seamlessly. ONLY the dark-brown leather grip wrap at the central handle remains non-gold.

adds.title: GOLD EDGE LINES TRACING EACH STEEL BOOT PLATE
adds.summary: Royal-payoff finishing beat on the boots — thin BUTTERY-GOLD edge lines (~0.15 inch thick) trace the outer boundary of each of the three steel boot plates: the L6 STEEL TOE CAP, the L9 STEEL HEEL CAP, and the L9 STEEL ANKLE GUARD BAND. Material accent on existing plates only — no new plate added, no surface trim band wrapping the leather cuff, no eyelets, no lacing. Steel plates underneath stay polished cool silver-gray.

prompt: EDIT GOAL — Take the archer in the input image (taller sharply-pointed conical steel helm with closed visor and the existing gold trim band at two-thirds height, polished steel breastplate with NO gold edge trim, steel vambraces with thin gold trim only at the wrist edge, taller layered two-plate steel pauldrons with clean steel-on-steel seam, FULL-ARM scale mail covering bicep through elbow through forearm with vambrace on top, red gambeson V-wedge at the throat center-front, red CLOTH MANTLE at the shoulders / back of neck, steel fauld plate skirt of 5-6 wider lames at the hip with clean steel edges, wider warm-brown leather waist belt with rectangular buttery-gold buckle and dark-wood dagger in a leather sheath at the anatomical-RIGHT hip (carries through from L3), leather greaves below the knee, brown leather boots with three steel plates carried over (steel toe cap from L6 + steel heel cap from L9 + steel ankle guard band from L9), brown back-quiver with white-feather red-tip arrows, FULL LONGBOW with outer-50% buttery-gold limb tips and warm wood-brown inner-50% limbs, dark-brown leather grip wrap at central handle, brass binding rings at each grip end, BARE peachy-tan hands, crimson cape, olive trousers) and apply FIVE Royal Elite finishing upgrades to produce the final L10 variant. The character is the same archer in the same calm hero-stance pose at roughly the same scale and framing — only the five beats below change. The L10 silhouette payoff is volume + material at the helm (cabasset brim + diamond finial cap), volume at the shoulders (bigger pauldrons for a mightier silhouette), full conversion of the longbow to gold, and gold edge lines tracing each of the boot's three steel plates. NO filigree, NO scrollwork, NO crown spikelets along the trim band, NO visor T-bar or visor perimeter trim, NO surface ornament beyond the explicit five beats. ===== NEW ADDS FOR THIS EDIT (royal payoff) ===== (1) HELM ADDS A SHORT FLARING CABASSET BRIM AT THE LOWER EDGE — the lower edge of the helm body now has a SHORT FLARING BRIM that curves slightly outward at the temple line and along the back of the neck (about 1 inch wide all around), giving the helm the classic 16th-century cabasset / pikeman-helm silhouette. CRITICAL — the TALL POINTED CONICAL APEX from the input image remains EXACTLY AS-IS in height and pointiness (do NOT shrink, dome, blunt, round off, or shorten the apex). The brim is added at the BOTTOM of the helm body only; the upper conical portion stays the same height and the same sharp taper as in the input image. The brim sits just above the eyes at the front and curves back over the ears and the back of the neck. The helm body remains polished cool silver-gray steel. (2) GOLD FACETED DIAMOND FINIAL CAPS THE TIP OF THE TALL POINTED APEX — a small (about 1.5 inch tall) FACETED BUTTERY-GOLD DIAMOND ornament now SITS AT THE VERY TIP of the helm's tall pointed conical apex, capping the sharp point. The tall pointed conical apex from the input image is PRESERVED in full — the diamond finial does NOT replace the apex, it CAPS it: the gold diamond is a small cubic / octahedral block of polished buttery-gold metal added on top of the existing tall point, with clean visible facets catching highlights. The total silhouette of the helm is now [tall pointed steel apex] + [small gold diamond cap at the very tip]. NOT a tall tapered spike, NOT a cone, NOT a sphere, NOT an egg, NOT a teardrop, NOT a rounded dome — the gold ornament itself is a clean faceted diamond / cube shape, BUT the steel apex below it remains tall and pointed exactly as in the input image. NO engraving, NO gemstones, NO bands wrapping the finial. GOLD RENDERING: saturated buttery-yellow base + soft top-down highlight reading almost white-yellow + deeper warm honey-orange shadow on the underside, IMMEDIATELY READABLE as gold at thumbnail scale. (3) PAULDRONS GROW BIGGER FOR A MIGHTIER SHOULDER SILHOUETTE — both layered two-plate steel pauldrons from the input image grow visibly LARGER and CHUNKIER for a mightier royal-rank shoulder silhouette. The volume bump applies to the EXISTING two plates (NOT a third lame): (a) the UPPER CAP DOME grows TALLER (extending ~1 inch further upward above the shoulder line) AND WIDER (the dome arcs further outward over the deltoid, broadening the shoulder silhouette by a clearly visible margin); (b) the LOWER LAME extends ~1 inch FURTHER DOWN the upper bicep, giving the pauldron a more substantial coverage profile. The clean steel-on-steel seam between the upper cap and the lower lame stays as a thin shadow line — NO new rivets, NO brass dots, NO gold trim, NO leading-edge piping along the seam or along the plate edges. Both plates remain the same polished cool silver-gray steel, with bright leading-edge highlights on the new larger upper cap dome. The pauldrons read DECISIVELY more massive than at the previous level — this is a pure VOLUME beat at the shoulder silhouette, no surface decoration. The new larger pauldrons may push slightly outboard of the previous shoulder silhouette but should not crowd the helm or the cape; the FULL-arm scale mail and the steel vambrace below remain unchanged. (4) FULLY BUTTERY-GOLD LONGBOW — the outer-50%-gilt longbow limbs from the input image FINISH their conversion to fully gold: the warm wood-brown inner-50% portion of each limb is now also polished BUTTERY YELLOW GOLD, matching the existing outer-50%-gilt portion seamlessly. The ENTIRE bow limb (both upper and lower limb, from limb tip to limb tip) is now polished buttery-gold metal, with a darker buttery-gold inner-curve shadow giving the limbs depth. Same gold rendering treatment as the helm beats — saturated buttery-yellow base + top-down highlight reading almost white-yellow + warm honey-orange shadow on the underside, immediately readable as gold at thumbnail scale. ONLY the dark-brown leather grip wrap at the central handle remains non-gold. The brass binding rings at each end of the leather grip wrap remain unchanged. The pale cream bowstring remains unchanged. The full longbow proportions and the bow's vertical hip-height position in the anatomical-LEFT hand remain unchanged. NO decorative scrollwork at limb tips, NO ornamental curls, NO etching — the bow limbs are CLEAN polished gold from tip to grip with the simple darker-gold inner-curve shadow only. (5) GOLD EDGE LINES TRACING EACH STEEL BOOT PLATE — royal-payoff finishing beat on the boots. The boots in the input image already wear THREE STEEL PLATES (the L6 TOE CAP at the front, the L9 HEEL CAP at the back, and the L9 ANKLE GUARD BAND wrapping each ankle horizontally just above the leather cuff). At this level, add a THIN BUTTERY-GOLD EDGE LINE (~0.15 inch thick) tracing the OUTER BOUNDARY of EACH of those three steel plates: (a) gold edge tracing the boundary of each STEEL TOE CAP where the steel meets the warm-brown leather body; (b) gold edge tracing the boundary of each STEEL HEEL CAP where the steel meets the leather; (c) gold edge tracing both the upper edge AND the lower edge of each STEEL ANKLE GUARD BAND where the steel meets the leather. Same gold rendering as the rest of the L10 gold accents — saturated buttery-yellow base + soft top-down highlight reading almost white-yellow + deeper warm honey-orange shadow on the underside. The steel plates underneath stay polished cool silver-gray; only the boundaries gain the gold edge. NO new plate added at L10 (no fourth boot plate, no shin plate). NO trim band wrapping the leather cuff. NO eyelets, NO lacing strings, NO stitching rows, NO surface ornament — boot accessory beats are PLATE accumulation and PLATE material change only. The boot's leather body remains chunky warm-brown leather with the darker folded ankle cuff. ===== PRESERVE FROM THE INPUT IMAGE EXACTLY ===== the closed full-face steel visor with the horizontal eye slit (face fully concealed — visor surface remains plain polished steel with NO new gold T-bar, NO new gold perimeter trim, NO new decoration); the gold helm trim band wrapping the helm's conical steel body at two-thirds height (carries through UNCHANGED — NO new crown spikelets along its upper edge, NO new ornaments along this band); the helm's conical steel body and TALL POINTED APEX (carries through unchanged, with the new short cabasset brim added at the lower edge per add 1 and the new gold diamond finial capping the apex tip per add 2 — the apex height and sharpness must NOT change); the red CLOTH MANTLE at the shoulders / back of the neck (the crimson-red mantle carried over from the input image, visible behind the helm's lower rim at the back of the neck); the polished steel breastplate body shape (UNCHANGED — NO new gold edge trim along the upper or lower edges, NO new filigree, NO new scrollwork, NO new gemstones; the breastplate stays plain polished steel as in the input image); the red gambeson V-wedge at the throat center-front between the closed visor's lower edge and the breastplate's upper edge (UNCHANGED in size, shape, color, position); both layered two-plate steel pauldrons (the input image's taller upper cap + lower lame stack — at this level the upper cap grows taller and wider AND the lower lame extends further down per add 3, but they remain the SAME TWO PLATES with the same clean steel-on-steel seam between them; NO new rivets, NO new leading-edge gold trim, NO new third lame is added); both steel vambraces from wrist to elbow with the existing thin BUTTERY GOLD trim line at each wrist edge (UNCHANGED — NO new etched scrollwork on the cuff, NO additional gold trim added beyond the existing wrist-edge band); the FULL-ARM scale mail covering each arm from deltoid down to wrist (chunky overlapping cool silver-gray scale plates per the established scale-mail visual spec) — visible at the bicep + elbow between the pauldron's lower lame and the steel vambrace's upper edge (NO bare-skin gap on either arm); both hands BARE peachy-tan skin (NO gauntlets, NO leather gloves, NO steel hand-plates — the hands stay bare exactly as in the input image); the FULL LONGBOW held vertically in his anatomical-LEFT hand at hip height (only the inner-50% gold conversion changes per add 4; the bow's full longbow proportions, vertical hip-height position, dark-brown leather grip wrap, brass binding rings, pale cream bowstring all unchanged); the brown leather cylindrical back-quiver slung diagonally across the back over his anatomical-LEFT shoulder rising behind the viewer-RIGHT side of the head, three to four arrows visible with WHITE feather fletching and RED bindings and RED nock tips and pale wood-tan shafts; the crimson-red knee-length cape attached at both shoulders falling with soft painted vertical folds (UNCHANGED — NO new gold cape clasps); the wider warm-brown leather waist belt with the existing rectangular buttery-gold buckle (UNCHANGED — the buckle remains plain rectangular gold, NOT cartouche-shaped, NOT etched); the dark-wood dagger in its leather sheath at the anatomical-RIGHT hip with the brass capstone on the handle (carries through unchanged — hangs from the wider belt); the steel fauld plate skirt at the hip (5-6 wider lames, polished cool silver-gray steel, clean lower edges with NO gold piping — UNCHANGED from input); the olive forest-green knee-length trousers visible above the greaves; the warm-brown leather wrap-style greaves on the lower legs from boot cuff to just below the knee with single cross-strap binding; the chunky warm-brown leather boots with the darker folded ankle cuff (carries through with: the L6 polished steel TOE CAP on each boot — now with the new gold edge line tracing its boundary per add 5; the L9 polished steel HEEL CAP on each boot — now with a gold edge line tracing its boundary; the L9 polished steel ANKLE GUARD BAND wrapping each boot at the ankle — now with gold edge lines on its upper and lower edges). ===== POSE (must match the input image exactly) ===== Three-quarter view, body angled approximately 15-25 degrees to the viewer's left so the character's anatomical right side faces camera, weight planted evenly on both feet with a very slight forward lean. THE BOW ARM (anatomical-LEFT, viewer-RIGHT) hangs DOWN at his side with the elbow only slightly bent, the bow held VERTICALLY at HIP height directly in front of the hip with the bare hand gripping the bow's central leather grip wrap. THE DRAW ARM (anatomical-RIGHT, viewer-LEFT) is bent sharply at the elbow at roughly 90 degrees, with the FOREARM HELD HORIZONTALLY ACROSS THE CHEST at upper-chest / sternum level (parallel to the ground). The vambraced bare hand sits at the CENTER OF THE CHEST area in front of the steel breastplate, slightly toward the bow side, in a TIGHT CLOSED FIST shape with the KNUCKLES facing OUTWARD toward the viewer. The fist does NOT touch the bow, the bowstring, or the breastplate. Calm balanced HERO STANCE; NOT an active draw, NOT a nocking motion, NOT a reach. ===== FRAMING / SCALE (must match these canonical numbers exactly) ===== 1024x1024 square canvas. Character occupies approximately 75% of canvas vertical height (~765px tall) and approximately 57% of canvas horizontal width (~580px wide). TOP of helm at approximately 13.5% from top of canvas (the new cabasset brim and the gold diamond cap may push slightly above the previous helm-tip position; allow up to 2-3% additional vertical extent if needed for the diamond cap). BOTTOM of boots at ~12% from bottom of canvas. Character roughly vertically centered, NOT bottom-anchored. DO NOT zoom in, DO NOT shrink, DO NOT add wider borders, DO NOT crop the helm or feet, DO NOT shift to bottom edge. ===== STYLE / OUTLINE ===== Supercell / Kingshot stylized mobile game art, hand-painted digital illustration with soft painted shading and visible brushwork, warm subsurface skin (where visible), saturated rich colors, warm shadow tones never gray, soft painted highlights on metal and leather and gold. Clean thin black silhouette outline around the entire character in deep warm brown-black with even weight, slight thickening in shadowed recesses, slight thinning under the key light. Interior linework soft and value-driven, never hard black. Clean white background with no environmental context, no ground plane, no scenery, minimal contact shadow. ===== NEGATIVE — DO NOT ===== DO NOT shrink, blunt, dome, round off, or shorten the helm's tall pointed conical apex from the input image — the apex height and sharpness must be PRESERVED EXACTLY; the gold diamond finial CAPS the tip and does NOT replace the apex; DO NOT make the gold finial a tall tapered cone, a tall spike, a sphere, an egg, a teardrop, or a rounded dome — it is a SHORT FACETED CUBIC / DIAMOND ornament with clean visible facets approximately 1.5 inches tall; DO NOT add bands, rings, gemstones, jewels, ornaments wrapping the finial; DO NOT make the cabasset brim wide like a kettle hat — it is a SHORT FLARING brim about 1 inch wide cabasset-style; DO NOT add three crown spikelets along the helm trim band — the trim band remains plain at this level; DO NOT add a vertical gold T-bar down the visor; DO NOT add gold perimeter trim around the visor edges; DO NOT add gold edge piping along the breastplate's upper or lower edges — the breastplate edges remain plain polished steel; DO NOT add filigree, fine engraving, etched scrollwork, fine vine motifs, flame motifs, or any ornamental gold lines anywhere on the breastplate, vambraces, pauldrons, fauld plates, or visor — every steel plate at this level remains plain polished steel apart from the existing helm gold trim band, the existing thin vambrace wrist-edge gold trim, and the new gold diamond finial cap + cabasset brim per adds 1-2; DO NOT add gold leading-edge trim along the front edge of either pauldron plate (upper cap or lower lame); DO NOT add a third smaller lame to the pauldrons — the pauldrons stay TWO-plate as in the input image; DO NOT add brass rivets, brass studs, gold dots, or any seam decoration along the pauldron seam; DO NOT add gold edge piping along the lower edge of any fauld lame — the fauld plates have CLEAN STEEL EDGES; DO NOT change the fauld plate count from 5-6 wider lames; DO NOT replace the rectangular gold belt buckle with a cartouche-shaped buckle, an ornamental shape, or an etched buckle — the buckle stays plain rectangular gold as in the input image; DO NOT add gold cape clasps at the shoulders — the cape attachment remains as in the input image with no new ornament; DO NOT add gauntleted gloves or steel hand-plates — both hands remain BARE peachy-tan skin exactly as in the input image; DO NOT add finger plates or articulated finger armor; DO NOT add metal sabatons or plate boots — boots remain warm-brown leather; DO NOT add a tabard, surcoat, or full-coat-of-arms cloth garment over the breastplate; DO NOT add a separate crown, tiara, or royal headpiece beyond the new diamond finial cap; DO NOT add wings, horns, dragon motifs, lion heads, sword-shaped clasps, or any over-the-top fantasy ornament; DO NOT add a sword, second weapon, scepter, or banner; DO NOT add gemstones, jewels, runes, glowing sigils, magical motifs, light rays, or luminous effects anywhere; DO NOT add decorative scrollwork at the bow limb tips — the bow limbs end in clean rounded gold tips (no curls, no flourishes, no leaf motifs); DO NOT replace the dark-brown leather grip wrap at the bow's central handle with gold — the leather grip wrap is the only non-gold portion of the bow; DO NOT add cloth tassets, red fabric strips, or yellow fabric strips at the hip — the steel fauld plate skirt remains; DO NOT keep any wood-brown color anywhere on the bow limbs — the entire bow limb (both upper and lower) is fully polished buttery gold; DO NOT remove the closed visor; DO NOT show any face above the breastplate (visor stays closed); DO NOT use FINE CHAINMAIL RING MESH, interlocking metal rings, fish-scale fine texture, or scale that reads as small dots — the FULL-arm scale mail visible at the bicep + elbow region between pauldron and vambrace is CHUNKY OVERLAPPING ROUNDED SCALES per the established visual spec; DO NOT shrink the scale mail back to short-sleeve at deltoid + upper bicep — at this level the scale mail is FULL-arm coverage carried over; DO NOT leave a bare-skin band at the bicep; DO NOT swap the hand holding the bow; DO NOT shorten the bow back from full longbow proportions; DO NOT change the chibi proportions; DO NOT change the cape color, length, or drape; DO NOT change the bow position; DO NOT change the arrow fletching colors (white feather + red tips); DO NOT add or remove arrows; DO NOT change the quiver position; DO NOT change the camera angle, framing, or composition; DO NOT crop the figure; DO NOT change the background or add scenery; DO NOT drift the art style; DO NOT change the aspect ratio from 1:1 square.
